3.2. Written Comments Submitted at the Meeting

The California Department of Transportation (Caltrans), working in cooperation with the Stanislaus
PROJECT
Council of Governments (StanCOG), proposes to construct a four-lane freeway/expressway along the
DESCRIPTION
adopted route south of Kansas Avenue from Dakota Avenue (Post Mile [PM] 11.0) to east of State
Route (SR) 99 at the Needham Street Bridge Overcrossing (PM 15.0). The total length of the SR-132.
West Freeway/Expressway Project would be approximately four miles and would include connections
on SR-99 from PM 15.7 to PM 17.5. The project involves phased construction of one of the two build
alternatives (Alt 1 or Alt 2) or the decision to implement the No-Build Alternative. The Caltrans Modesto
Soil Stockpiles occupy three areas within state right-of-way south of Kansas Avenue. Caltrans
proposes to cover or enclose the soil stockpiles within the SR-132 West Freeway/Expressway
construction project as detailed in the Draft Final Remedial Action Plan (RAP).
o Improve regional and interregional circulation in Modesto and Stanislaus County
PURPOSE

    Relieve traffic congestion along existing SR-132 (Maze Boulevard)

O Improve traffic operations for the existing and proposed transportation network
The deficiencies of the existing highway and increases in regional and interregional traffic are
NEED
anticipated to result in continued traffic congestion. The proposed project would relieve traffic
congestion along existing SR-132 (Maze Boulevard) because the existing SR-132 (Maze Boulevard)
currently experiences, and would continue to be burdened by increased traffic. The proposed project
would also improve operations for the existing and proposed transportation network because the
operational efficiency is reduced by the proximity and direct access to schools, churches, businesses,
and residences by way of existing driveways along existing SR-132 (Maze Boulevard).

    Final Environmental Document

TIMELINE
O Design, Right of Way, and Permitting.....
2017-2018
(approximate)
O Start Construction of Phase 1
